Scenario.overview: Water and Wastewater Systems Simple This scenario outlines a targeted cyber attack against water and wastewater systems by the 'FunnyDream' group, employing a sophisticated yet straightforward approach. The attackers focus on initial access through supply chain compromise, leveraging valid accounts for execution, and causing impact via data destruction.
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Delivery Method, User Account Authentication (Mechanism), File Modification (Mechanism)

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 439; Name: Manipulation During Distribution
stage.description: The attackers infiltrate the water and wastewater systems by compromising suppliers or contractors, manipulating their products or services before delivery, thus gaining initial access to the target network.

stage.name: Execution
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 645; Name: Use of Captured Tickets (Pass The Ticket)
stage.description: Utilizing credentials obtained from the supply chain compromise, the attackers execute commands and actions on the compromised systems using legitimate user accounts.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 675; Name: Retrieve Data from Decommissioned Devices
stage.description: The attackers cause significant disruption by destroying critical data and overwriting essential system files, leading to operational downtime and potential safety hazards.

Scenario.summary: The 'FunnyDream' group employs a streamlined but effective strategy targeting water and wastewater systems. They gain entry through supply chain compromises, leverage valid accounts for execution, and ultimately achieve their goal of disrupting operations by destructively impacting critical data.
